title: 4.3 Binary Tree Basics
subtitle: Binary tree node structure, key properties, and BST foundation

content.introduction: Tree fundamentals define hierarchical data modeling with parent-child relationships and are the basis for many indexing and search structures.

sections.title: 4.3.1 Key Ideas and Model
sections.content: Define node, edge, root, leaf, degree, depth, and height. Explain subtree relations and recursive structure.
examples: Identify root, internal nodes, and leaves in a sample tree
Compute node depth and tree height

sections.title: 4.3.2 Representation and Workflow
sections.content: Describe common tree representations and traversal perspectives. Explain how hierarchy captures nested or ordered relationships.
examples: Represent a tree with linked nodes
Represent a rooted tree with parent arrays
Interpret a filesystem as a tree model

sections.title: 4.3.3 Complexity and Practical Guidance
sections.content: Discuss operation cost trends with respect to height, and why balanced trees are preferred for predictable performance.
examples: Explain how height impacts search cost
Compare skewed and balanced tree behavior
Choose tree structures for hierarchical data

keyTakeaways: Relate tree height to operation efficiency
Prepare for binary and balanced tree algorithms
